# Study Material Format Instructions (Persistent Workspace Rules)

Use these rules for every Java program study file in this workspace.

## Goal
Create beginner-friendly study material that is very easy to read and understand without prior programming experience.

## File Organization
- Keep separate files for each program.
- Keep Markdown files in `Program_Wise_Study_Material/md/`.
- Keep plain text files in `Program_Wise_Study_Material/txt/`.
- Keep the same content structure in both `.md` and `.txt` versions.

## Mandatory Content Order (for each program file)
1. Program title
2. Java code block with inline comments
3. Variables Used (Easy Meaning)
4. Simple understanding (short flow bullets)
5. Input Structure (How to Enter)
6. Sample Input / Output (categorized)
7. Actual Input (Raw Console)
8. Actual Output (Raw Console)
9. Output Explanation (Easy)

## Writing Style Requirements
- Use very simple English.
- Keep sentences short and clear.
- Avoid complex technical terms; if needed, explain in simple words.
- Make analogies practical and easy (school, lock-key, real life).

## Code Explanation Requirements
- Explain directly in code comments next to lines (inline), not only in separate paragraphs.
- Clearly explain:
  - method purpose (`main`, etc.)
  - each variable purpose
  - loops (`for`, `while`) and why they are used
  - conditions (`if`, `switch`) and what happens when true/false
  - why the output appears in that format
- Add beginner-friendly input/output prompt messages where appropriate.
- Close `Scanner` objects with a simple explanation comment.

## Input/Output Formatting Requirements
- Show input in categorized form first (step-by-step labels).
- Also show raw console input separately.
- Show raw console output separately.
- Keep sample values realistic and consistent with code logic.
- Include invalid scenario output when useful (for understanding errors/conditions).

## Consistency Rules
- Use the same section headings in every program.
- Keep explanation depth similar across all programs.
- When one file is updated, mirror the same style in its matching `.txt` file.

## User Preference Reminder
The user should not have to repeat these requirements again.
Always follow these instructions for next programs by default.
